summary: Stancil argues that today’s tech/AI wealth numbers are so vast they’ve broken our ability to make sense of them, yet we can’t stop doing the math because it fuels comparison and anxiety. The industry’s old story that money is a footnote has flipped—valuation headlines and compensation gaps now dominate the psychological landscape. He warns that even if AI delivers abundance, social comparison may keep “enough” out of reach.
money_quotes: I do know one thing though: Never do the math.
We don’t do the math to measure ourselves; we do the math to compare ourselves.
But even during the prior boom years, the footnotes were already beginning to overtake the main text.
Dizzying wealth is no longer just in the corner office of the top floor; it’s now down the hall, at the desk next to you.
If we build a machine that can give us everything, when do we dismantle the machine that makes us doubt that it is enough?
key_insight: The math of modern tech wealth turns abundance into anxiety by making comparison unavoidable, so even AI-driven prosperity may not deliver a sense of enough.
